definition: Ethical conduct or disciplined behavior that steadies and supports the path.
notes: The project prefers conduct because it stays close to lived behavior and avoids the abstract, often preachy tone of morality. Ethical conduct is acceptable when the moral dimension needs to be explicit, while virtue can work in compact list contexts but is less preferable as a default. The OSF glossary explains s?la in moral-conduct language, which the repository records as standard alternate explanatory vocabulary while retaining conduct as the default.

context_rules.context: In most practical and doctrinal contexts
In explanatory prose emphasizing the ethical dimension of behavior
context_rules.notes: Acceptable alternate when plain conduct feels too broad.

context_rules.context: In compact doctrinal lists where a short noun is needed

example_phrases.notes: Useful expression showing sīla as embodied practice.
